A member in the trading room was asking me about $INOD yesterday, and I explained it could work, but wasn’t for me - this is due to the megaphone pattern.

In other words, volatility is expanding, not contracting.

You know what... I have a fun challenge:

Explain your trading system in the least amount of words possible.

I'll go first: "Relative Strength"

If it takes 17 indicators, 4 monitors, and a 45min TED Talk to explain, you might be overcomplicating it.

My 2 cents.

/ES free fall into the close only for them to rip it back after hours

Classic example of how market is designed to take your money

This is why patience is so important, and why a good cost basis can assist patience

I've been getting a lot of questions lately on: 

'what do you think of [Ticker]' / 'what trades are you thinking of doing/did you take any trades' / 'why no interest in shorting [Ticker]?'

1) The market environment has shifted right after a strong momentum market in April +

I Can't Answer Your Question

When I received a query on 'what do you think of [Ticker]', from my experience (not to say that I receive such question a lot) people who asked this type of question fall into either one of the following categories:

1) A noob trader who just heard

Good morning! 

Great post to start your day with from Jackson. 

You MUST have a system, a process, a set of rules to lean on.

Days like yesterday could have you selling everything, only to watch in despair as things rip right back. 

Without your stocks having done anything
@FranVezz @PurpleDrink_LLC I like to call it fed reversal day 😂
Getting the market direction right and staying in the right sector, group and then the strongest names in that group is the secret sauce to trading.

The phrase "buy low, sell high" has probably cost retail billions of dollars.

People hear "buy low" & immediately start digging through the market's trash can looking for bargains.

But institutions are buying the strongest names in the strongest groups.

Funny how that works!
@ohiain Buy higher, seller higher. This is the way!
